target: Section 3.5.4 in data/sections.json part3b — remove misplaced WIN-024 paragraph, add bridging text

patch_target.extraction_method: Replace all content between '<h2>3.5.4' and '<h2>3.5.5' (exclusive of the 3.5.5 heading)

analysis:
WIN-024 (Roaring 40s = SAA boundary) is placed in section 3.5.4 'Globe Values Relabeled as Dome Predictions,' but WIN-024 has code_analysis.relabels_standard=false. The dome's WIN-024 claim is NOT a relabeling case — it does not take a standard physics formula and rename the mechanism. Instead, it asserts a causal connection between two geographically unrelated phenomena (the SAA and the Roaring 40s) based on a latitude coincidence. This is a spurious correlation, not vocabulary substitution.

Additional problems with the current WIN-024 paragraph in 3.5.4:
1. States 'SAA southern boundary ~30–35°S' which contradicts WIN-024's own detail entry (~45–50°S at 32,000 nT contour) — the curmudgeon flagged this imprecision in Cycle 1.
2. Lacks the longitude mismatch argument, Ferrel cell physics, monitor.py code analysis, and Cnossen 2016 acknowledgment added to WIN-024's detail entry via EXP-007.
3. The paragraph is the weakest example in the section — three genuine relabeling cases (tidal periods spanning 5 WINs, Hubble Law, P-wave shadow zone) all have relabels_standard=true and are much stronger illustrations of the section's thesis.

The fix: remove the WIN-024 paragraph and add a bridging paragraph at the end that (a) notes relabeling is not the only Misleading pattern, (b) names WIN-024 as an example of a different pattern (spurious geographic coincidence), and (c) cross-references the detail table so no reader thinks we removed a claim rather than recategorized it.
replacement_html:
3.5.4 Globe Values Relabeled as Dome Predictions

Multiple WINs claim observations that are well-established in standard physics, presenting them as dome predictions by renaming the mechanism. Code analysis of the full repository (see Part 4.6) shows {{CA_RELABELS}} of {{TOTAL_WINS}} WINs (nearly three-quarters of all claims) relabel standard physics as "aetheric" phenomena — the single most common structural pattern in the model. The examples below illustrate the pattern across different scientific domains:

Tidal periods (WIN-045, 046, 049, 050, 051): These five WINs claim the M2 (12.42 hr), S2 (12.00 hr), K1 (23.93 hr), O1 (25.82 hr), and N2 (12.66 hr) tidal constituent periods. These are astronomical constants known since Laplace (1775) and George Darwin (1883). They follow from the periods of the Moon's orbit and Earth's rotation — any model that includes a moon and a rotating reference frame reproduces them automatically. Claiming them as dome "predictions" is equivalent to predicting the length of a day. All five were added in V51.0, registered on 2026-04-05 — hours before this critical review was launched — a batch expansion of the WIN count using well-established constants that add no discriminating power. Strategic timing: adopting nineteenth-century tidal constants as new "predictions" on the eve of an independent audit raises the question of whether these WINs were added to inflate the count rather than because the dome model genuinely predicts them. Transparency note: all five tidal WINs derive from a single underlying geometric constraint — the dome's local moon at 2,534 km altitude cannot produce the observed two-bulge semidiurnal tidal pattern regardless of the tidal mechanism invoked (see Section 4.5.4). They are counted as five separate self-contradictions in the verdict tally because the dome registers them as five separate WINs, but the reader should understand they reflect one independent geometric failure.

Hubble Law (WIN-047): Uses z = D/λ_A where λ_A = c/H₀ = 4,283 Mpc. This is literally Hubble's Law with the standard globe-cosmology value H₀ = 70 km/s/Mpc. The dome model has no galactic-scale mechanism — it describes a local cavity ~20,000 km across. Claiming a cosmological distance-redshift relation from a dome model that cannot see past its own firmament is a category error. The formula is borrowed, not derived.

P-wave shadow zone (WIN-064): Claims a "Sub-Terrestrial Wall" at the dome's bottom boundary creates the seismic shadow zone at 104°–140°. The problem: the P-wave shadow zone is observed at all azimuths from any earthquake — it forms a perfect ring around the epicenter regardless of direction. A wall at a fixed geographic location (the dome's bottom plate) would cast a shadow in specific directions, not a symmetric ring. The globe explanation (refraction through a spherical liquid core) naturally produces the observed azimuthal symmetry. The dome's proposed mechanism is geometrically incompatible with the observation it claims to explain.

Relabeling is the most common structural pattern in the dome model, but it is not the only way a claim earns a "Misleading" verdict. Other patterns include spurious geographic coincidences — for example, WIN-024 claims the Roaring 40s wind belt and the SAA share a cause because both fall near 40–50°S, but the Roaring 40s encircle the entire Southern Hemisphere while the SAA spans only ~130° of longitude (see the detail analysis). Others involve circular derivations or count-inflating duplications. The individual WIN analyses in Part 2 document each case.
changes_summary: Removed WIN-024 paragraph from section 3.5.4 (spurious correlation, not relabeling — code_analysis.relabels_standard=false)
Removed stale 'SAA southern boundary ~30–35°S' text that contradicted WIN-024's own detail entry (~45–50°S at 32,000 nT contour)
Added bridging paragraph at section end distinguishing relabeling from other Misleading patterns (spurious correlations, circular derivations, count inflation)
Bridging paragraph names WIN-024 explicitly and cross-references the detail table, so no reader thinks the analysis was removed
Preserved all three genuinely relabeling examples unchanged (tidal periods, Hubble Law, P-wave shadow zone) — all have relabels_standard=true
holes_addressed: ISS-302: WIN-024 structural misplacement (relabels_standard=false in relabeling section)
Stale SAA latitude range (~30–35°S vs actual ~45–50°S) — eliminated by removing the paragraph
Section implicitly claimed all Misleading WINs are relabeling cases — bridging paragraph now distinguishes pattern types
anticipated_objections: Reader may wonder where WIN-024 went — bridging paragraph explicitly names it with a cross-reference link to the detail analysis

Removing an example makes the section shorter — but the section's job is to illustrate relabeling, and three strong examples (tidal periods spanning 5 WINs, Hubble Law, P-wave) are sufficient; a misplaced example weakens the section's coherence

The bridging paragraph introduces categories (spurious correlations, circular derivations, count inflation) that could be seen as needing their own subsections — addressed by pointing to Part 2's per-WIN detail, where each case is fully documented

A dome defender might argue WIN-024 IS relabeling because it relabels Ferrel cell circulation as 'aetheric rim pressure' — but the key claim in WIN-024 is the CAUSAL CONNECTION between SAA and winds, not the mechanism label. The dome isn't saying 'Ferrel cell is aetheric'; it's saying 'SAA CAUSES the Roaring 40s.' That's a causal claim, not a vocabulary swap.
kernel_of_truth: WIN-024 does identify a real latitude overlap (Roaring 40s at 40–50°S, SAA boundary at ~45–50°S). The dome's error is claiming causation from coincidence, not relabeling standard physics. Correctly categorizing this distinction in the section structure makes our taxonomy more honest and harder to attack. If we mix up 'relabeling' and 'spurious correlation' in the same section, a careful reader will notice the inconsistency — exactly the kind of sloppiness we criticize in the dome model.
note_on_existing_file: The previous EXP-009.json contains misrouted SEC-3.1/SEC-4.6 content from a prior run. That content was integrated via EXP-004. This file (EXP-009-sec354.json) is the actual EXP-009 expansion addressing the WIN-024 structural misplacement in Section 3.5.4.
